claim: Herbal pages describe Galenic pharmaceutical properties (kyānā 'nature', ṭārā 'press/apply') rather than botanical catalogs — adding Löw botanical entries (42 terms) increased herbal coverage by only 0.1pp, and top decoded words match pharma page vocabulary.
evidence_for: 0.1pp coverage change from 42-term botanical supplement is low ceiling
kyānā enriched 3.3x on herbal pages, ṭārā 3.2x — suggests pharma vocabulary
evidence_against: Top-word overlap could also reflect shared function-word vocabulary
The 0.1pp test is contingent on lexicon completeness
test: Compare decoded top-10 words on herbal vs pharma folios for overlap percentage
tests_remaining: Compare decoded top-10 words on herbal vs pharma folios for overlap percentage
reasoning: Requires Brady pipeline output to fully test.
source: Brady Defibaugh (April 2026), A Syriac Pharmaceutical Dispensatory Encoded in the Voynich Manuscript (DANI pipeline v31)
